title: possibly lower HDL cholesterol in women
summary: possibly lower HDL cholesterol in women
detail: One report indicates that women (but not men) with this genotype may have slightly lower levels of HDL cholesterol, but other reports have not observed this. title: Faster aging. Increased risk for Dementia. Less good cholesterol
summary: Faster aging. Increased risk for Dementia. Less good cholesterol.
detail: This reduces HDL ("good") cholesterol. HDL cholesterol removes cholesterol from artery walls and improves health. This SNP seems to be associated with higher blood pressure, less longevity, and more diseases of aging, in particular memory decline and dementia, and lower odds of living to 100, especially in Ashkenazi Jews. Attempting to raise HDL cholesterol may (or may not) be useful to mitigate the effects of this SNP.
whyItMatters: SNPedia has a genotype-specific page for this result, which can add consumer-facing context alongside primary evidence sources.

title: Lower risk of dementia and Alzheimer's disease. Higher good cholesterol
summary: Lower risk of dementia and Alzheimer's disease. Higher good cholesterol.

title: Longer lifespan, 0.28x lower risk of dementia, 0.31x lower risk of Alzheimer's
summary: Longer lifespan, 0.28x lower risk of dementia, 0.31x lower risk of Alzheimer's.
detail: This SNP has been related to exceptional long life. A study in Ashkenazi Jews showed that individuals with this genotype have significantly longer lifespans, and better cognitive function. ) Another study shows that this homozygote has an average 70% lower risk of dementia and Alzheimer's disease. This SNP might work by raising HDL ("good") cholesterol and lowering blood pressure.
whyItMatters: SNPedia has a genotype-specific page for this result, which can add consumer-facing context alongside primary evidence sources.
